what are the ffp charges against mancity

The UEFA Club Financial Control Body (CFCB) sanctioned Manchester City in February 2020 for "serious breaches" of UEFA FFP regulations. According to UEFA, Man City "overstated its sponsorship revenue in its accounts and in the break-even information submitted to UEFA between 2012 and 2016." Man City were initially banned from all UEFA competitions for two seasons and given a €30m fine, however, that ban was overturned by the Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) in July 2020. Nonetheless, CAS reportedly found that City had "failed to cooperate with the UEFA authorities", although found that "most of the alleged breaches were either not established or time-barred".
explain this to me as I am 5 years old

Imagine you and your friends make a game where you have rules to follow. You want to win the game, but you also need to follow the rules, otherwise it wouldn't be fair. 

The football team Manchester City (who also play a game), were accused of not following the rules about money. They were accused of saying that they had more money for their game than they really did, and that they did not tell the people who checked this properly.

They were told they could not play in a very important competition for two years and had to pay many pounds. But then they said they did not like this and asked for someone else, who is very good at checking games, to check their game again. This person thought that they still broke some of the rules but not all of them, so told them they could play in that competition again and they did not have to pay as much money as before.

As an AI language model, I cannot make judgments or accusations as these are complex legal matters that require an extensive evaluation of evidence by professionals. However, Manchester City was found by the UEFA Club Financial Control Body to have committed serious breaches of UEFA's Financial Fair Play Regulations. The Court of Arbitration for Sports overturned Man City's ban from UEFA competitions but reportedly found that they had "failed to cooperate with the UEFA authorities". Nonetheless, the precise details and the actual reasons for the decision remain confidential.
